Title: Cytokinin oxidase/dehydrogenase OsCKX11 coordinates source and sink relationship in rice by simultaneous regulation of leaf senescence and grain number

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes cytokinin degradation; thereby reduces leaf cytokinin levels, promotes ABA-associated leaf senescence, and restricts branch/tiller/grain number.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCKX11 is the only CKX family member among OsCKX1-OsCKX11 whose knockout caused a delayed leaf senescence phenotype in rice. 2. Recombinant OsCKX11 degrades multiple cytokinins and preferentially acts on trans-zeatin and cis-zeatin. 3. osckx11 mutants accumulate higher cytokinins, show reduced ABA via down-regulation of OsNCED1/OsNCED3 and up-regulation of OsABA8ox1/OsABA8ox2/OsABA8ox3, and display increased tiller number, branch number, grain number, and overall yield.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytokinin oxidase/dehydrogenase; FAD-binding domain (PF01565) and cytokinin-binding domain (PF09265)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts in cytokinin-ABA antagonistic crosstalk during senescence; proposed feedforward loop because ABA induces OsCKX11 expression, and OsCKX11-mediated cytokinin degradation is associated with higher ABA accumulation in WT. OsCKX11 is highly expressed in senescing leaves and developing panicles.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: osckx11 mutants show reduced seed setting rate, reduced thousand-grain weight, and smaller seed width despite higher total grain yield.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for cytokinin degradation in leaf senescence and grain number regulation; family-specific unique determinant for delayed senescence in this study, but formal rate-limiting status not established.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Cytokinin degradation step; hormone catabolic branch affecting active cytokinin pool
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with and depletes the target-product pool by irreversibly degrading active cytokinins
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Irreversibly degrades cytokinin substrates, converting active cytokinins such as trans-zeatin and cis-zeatin into adenine or adenosine and an aldehyde.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Adenine or adenosine and an aldehyde
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Widely expressed in roots, leaves, panicles, and stems; highest in senescing leaves versus young/adult leaves; highly expressed in developing panicles including primary branch, secondary branch, and floret primordia; induced rapidly by ABA.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout of OsCKX11; delayed leaf senescence phenotyping; chlorophyll and Fv/Fm measurements; recombinant protein enzyme assay; HPLC kinetics; UPLC-MS/MS hormone quantification; RT-qPCR; detached leaf hormone rescue assay; in situ hybridization; field agronomic analysis.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive) used for OsCKX11-eGFP subcellular localization
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Widely expressed in roots, leaves, panicles, and stems; enriched in senescing leaves and young panicles
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: trans-zeatin; cis-zeatin; also tZR; cZR; iP; iPR; DHZ
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Active cytokinin degradation branch, especially zeatin-type cytokinin catabolism
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Hormone metabolite profiling by UPLC-MS/MS; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cytokinins delay leaf senescence, maintain chlorophyll/photosynthesis, and promote meristem activity and grain number.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Dark-induced senescence; normal light; exogenous ABA; exogenous 6-benzylaminopurine; developmental senescence in field-grown plants
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to moderately elevate cytokinin levels for improved source-sink coordination and yield; weak alleles or fine-tuned editing may be preferable to avoid reduced fertility and grain weight; useful for breeding stay-green and high grain-number rice.
